perturbation.original_text: "Trade Secrets" means all forms and types of financial, business, scientific, technical, economic or engineering information, including patterns, plans, compilations, program devices, formulas, designs, prototypes, methods, techniques, processes, procedures, programs or codes, whether tangible or intangible, and whether or how stored, compiled or memorialized physically, electronically, graphically, photographically or in writing, to the extent that the owner thereof has taken reasonable measures to keep such information secret and the information derives independent economic value, actual or potential, from not being generally known to, and not being readily ascertainable through proper means by, the public.
perturbation.changed_text: "Trade Secrets" means all forms and types of financial, business, scientific, technical, economic or engineering information, including patterns, plans, compilations, program devices, formulas, designs, prototypes, methods, techniques, processes, procedures, programs or codes, whether tangible or intangible, and whether or how stored, compiled or memorialized physically, electronically, graphically, photographically or in writing, regardless of the measures taken by the owner to keep such information secret or whether the information derives independent economic value.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the requirement that the owner must take reasonable measures to keep the information secret and that the information must derive independent economic value to be considered a trade secret. This directly contradicts Section 3.02(d) and 4.02(d) which requires SpinCo and Honeywell to move trade secrets in tangible form prior to distribution date. The original definition created an uncertainty as it will be difficult to ascertain at what level or degree the owner should protect the Trade Secret, however the new definition is now ambiguous as it doesn't required any measurement from the Trade Secret, therefore creating the in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) All SpinCo Trade Secrets shall be in or shall be moved to the physical possession of the SpinCo Group in a tangible form (including all copies thereof and all notes, extracts or summaries based thereon) prior to the Distribution Date. At the specific written request of SpinCo, Honeywell shall destroy or shall have destroyed any form or copy of any SpinCo Trade Secrets specified by SpinCo in such written request that are in the possession of Honeywell or any members of the Honeywell Group and were not used in the Honeywell Business as of immediately prior to the Distribution, other than SpinCo Trade Secrets that were electronically preserved or recorded by an electronic backup system prior to the Distribution Date and remain within a secure, encrypted data backup system that is subject to industry practice defense, protection and access restriction measures.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of "Trade Secrets" by removing the requirement that the owner take reasonable measures to keep the information secret and that the information derive independent economic value. This directly contradicts section (d) of the contradicted text, which obligates SpinCo and Honeywell to move or destroy "SpinCo Trade Secrets" in tangible form prior to the Distribution Date. Because the changed text expands the definition of Trade Secret it will be difficult to ascertain what needs to be destroyed or moved because no measures need to be taken to classify something a Trade Secret, thus, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (a) General. The Parties acknowledge that through the course of a history of integrated operations SpinCo and the members of the SpinCo Group have each obtained knowledge of and access to, or otherwise used, certain Honeywell IP, including Patents, Trade Secrets, copyrighted content, proprietary know-how, and other Intellectual Property Rights that are not otherwise governed expressly by the Separation Agreement or the Ancillary Agreements or identified expressly in the schedules thereto (collectively, "Honeywell Shared IP"). With regard
perturbation.changed_text: (a) General. The Parties acknowledge that through the course of a history of integrated operations SpinCo and the members of the SpinCo Group may have gained limited knowledge of and occasional access to, or infrequently used, certain Honeywell IP, including Patents, Trade Secrets, copyrighted content, proprietary know-how, and other Intellectual Property Rights that are not otherwise governed expressly by the Separation Agreement or the Ancillary Agreements or identified expressly in the schedules thereto (collectively, "Honeywell Shared IP"). With regard
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'have each obtained knowledge of and access to, or otherwise used' to 'may have gained limited knowledge of and occasional access to, or infrequently used' introduces ambiguity regarding the extent of SpinCo's prior usage of Honeywell Shared IP. This weakens the basis for the license grant and creates an in-text contradiction and challenges Section 4.01(a), where it requires Honeywell has to have freedom to use the SpinCo shared IP. The original contract indicated that Honeywell had freedom to use, in section 4.01, due to the prior integrated operations. The perturbation is introducing an uncertainty that will make difficult to prove the Honeywell had access to the SpinCo Shared IP.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) General. The Parties acknowledge that through the course of a history of integrated operations Honeywell and the members of the Honeywell Groups have each obtained knowledge of and access to, or otherwise used, certain SpinCo IP, including Patents, Trade Secrets, copyrighted content, proprietary know-how, and other Intellectual Property Rights that are not otherwise governed expressly by the Separation Agreement or the Ancillary Agreements or identified expressly in the schedules thereto (collectively, "SpinCo Shared IP"). With regard to the SpinCo Shared IP, the Parties seek to ensure that Honeywell has the freedom to use such SpinCo Shared IP in the future. Hence, as of the Distribution Date, SpinCo hereby grants, and agrees to cause the members of the SpinCo Group to hereby grant, to Honeywell and the members of the Honeywell Group a non-exclusive, royalty-free, fully-paid, perpetual, sublicenseable (solely to Subsidiaries and suppliers for "have made" purposes), worldwide license to use and exercise rights under the SpinCo Shared IP (excluding Trademarks and the subject matter of any other Ancillary Agreement), said license being limited to use of a similar type, scope and extent as used in the Honeywell Business prior to the Distribution Date and the natural growth and development thereof.
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the original assertion of knowledge and access to 'Honeywell Shared IP' by SpinCo, changing 'have each obtained knowledge of and access to, or otherwise used' to 'may have gained limited knowledge of and occasional access to, or infrequently used.' This change contradicts the requirement in the 'Contradicted Text' section (Section 4.01(a)) that Honeywell has the freedom to use 'SpinCo Shared IP' because the original contract indicated that Honeywell had freedom to use due to the prior integrated operations, implying shared knowledge and usage. By weakening this premise in the modified section, it introduces ambiguity that makes it more difficult to justify Honeywell's freedom to use the 'SpinCo Shared IP', creating a direct contradiction. This aligns with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Section 8.01. No Assignment or Transfer Without Consent. Except as expressly set forth in this Agreement, neither this Agreement nor any of the rights, interests or obligations under this Agreement, including the licenses granted pursuant to this Agreement, shall be assigned, in whole or in part, by operation of Law or otherwise by either Party without the prior written consent of the other Party. Any purported assignment without such consent shall be void.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 8.01. No Assignment or Transfer Without Consent. Except as expressly set forth in this Agreement, neither this Agreement nor any of the rights, interests or obligations under this Agreement, including the licenses granted pursuant to this Agreement, can be assigned, in whole or in part, by operation of Law or otherwise by either Party without making reasonable efforts to get the prior written consent of the other Party. Any purported assignment without such consent shall be voidable.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall be assigned' to 'can be assigned' and 'without the prior written consent of the other Party' to 'without making reasonable efforts to get the prior written consent of the other Party' and 'void' to 'voidable' introduces uncertainty regarding the assignment's validity. This weakens the enforceability of the no-assignment clause. This creates an in-text contradiction if other sections, such as dispute resolution (Section 11.02), rely on the strict enforceability of all terms in the agreement. The perturbation has introduced ambiguity as it is up to interpretation to what constitutes making reasonable efforts, this ambiguity clashes with the original text in dispute resolution
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 11.02. Dispute Resolution. In the event that either Party, acting reasonably, forms the view that another Party has caused a material breach of the terms of this Agreement, then the Party that forms such a view shall serve written notice of the alleged breach on the other Parties and the Parties shall work together in good faith to resolve any such alleged breach within 12 Source: GARRETT MOTION INC., 8-K, 10/1/2018 Table of Contents thirty (30) days of such notice (a "Dispute"). If any such alleged breach is not so resolved, then a senior executive of each Party shall, in good faith, attempt to resolve any such alleged breach within the following thirty (30) days of the referral of the matter to the senior executives. If no resolution is reached with respect to any such alleged breach in accordance with the procedures contained in this Section 11.02, then the Parties may seek to resolve such matter in accordance with Section 11.03, Section 11.04 and Section 11.06
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall be assigned' to 'can be assigned' and 'prior written consent' to 'making reasonable efforts to get the prior written consent' and 'void' to 'voidable' introduces significant ambiguity regarding the enforceability of the no-assignment clause, which is inconsistent with Section 11.02, which assumes strict enforcement of the agreement's terms. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' as the changed text makes interpretation unclear, potentially affecting dispute resolution based on a 'material breach of the terms of this Agreement'.
